mnemonics.title: Disk Access Time Components
mnemonics.content: SEEK + ROTATE + TRANSFER = ACCESS TIME. REMEMBER: SRT - SEEK TIME IS THE SLOWEST (STARTS WITH S)

mnemonics.title: Disk Scheduling Order of Performance
mnemonics.content: BEST: C-SCAN / LOOK | GOOD: SCAN / SSTF | WORST: FCFS. MNEMONIC: CANS (C-SCAN) ARE LOOKED AT BEFORE SCAN, BEFORE SSTF, BEFORE FCFS

mnemonics.title: Disk Geometry Terms
mnemonics.content: TRACK (CIRCLE) -> SECTOR (PIE SLICE) -> CYLINDER (STACK OF CIRCLES). THINK OF A TIRE: TRACK = TIRE CIRCLE, SECTOR = SPOKE, CYLINDER = ALL TIRES TOGETHER

acronyms.usage: Simplest disk scheduling algorithm, processes requests in arrival order
Disk scheduling that prioritizes closest requests to reduce head movement
Disk scheduling moving from one end to other, servicing all requests in path
Disk scheduling that cycles through all tracks providing uniform wait times
Data in sector used to detect and correct read/write errors
First sector containing boot loader and partition table
Measure of disk rotational speed affecting access time
Technology combining multiple disks for reliability and performance

visualTips.visualization: Imagine stacking multiple CDs on top of each other - all the holes align and the laser can access any 'track' on any CD without moving. That vertical stack of tracks is a cylinder.
Think of a pizza cut into slices - each slice is a sector. The crust is the outer track. Each slice (sector) holds a fixed amount of pizza data.

SCAN: Like an elevator going UP servicing everyone on floors 1-10, then DOWN servicing 9-1. C-SCAN: Like a carousel - everyone gets served in order as the horse passes, then starts over from beginning.
The disk arm is like a record player needle - moving it to a different track takes time. Moving across many tracks takes longer than moving to an adjacent track.

analogies.analogy: Secondary storage is like a filing cabinet (permanent, holds everything) while primary storage (RAM) is like your desk surface (fast access but limited space, stuff gets cleared when you leave).

Think of a grocery store's shelf restocking. Instead of going to the warehouse (disk) every time someone buys milk, the store keeps some in the back room (cache/RAM) for quick access. Much faster but limited space.

Imagine a delivery truck in a city. FCFS is like following the exact order customers called in (might zigzag everywhere). SCAN is like going down one street completely, then reversing and going down the next. Efficient and fair.

analogies.analization: Like waiting for a rotating revolving door to align with your floor - you have to wait for it to rotate to just the right position before you can get on (or read your data).
